steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage presents two distinct benefits of community science: it promotes conservation engagement and it greatly expands data collection. The correct answer must capture both benefits accurately.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Grace Herzel...surprised at the effect...': Focuses on a single researcher's reaction to one study rather than summarizing the passage's overall claim about community science. ✗
  • Choice B — 'less likely to help researchers obtain accurate data': Directly contradicts the passage, which states that community science greatly increases data collection. ✗
  • Choice C — 'increase conservation efforts and generate large amounts of data': Captures both benefits the passage identifies — community engagement through conservation and large-scale data generation for researchers. ✓
  • Choice D — 'unlikely to have much of an effect on conservation efforts': Also contradicts the passage, which presents increased conservation efforts as a key benefit. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
